headline: Trump Says the Energy Shock Will Be Short-Lived. CEOs Paint a Scarier Picture.
summary: Some executives are privately expressing frustration with the administration’s optimistic messaging and say the disruption is already far-reaching.

content.title: Trump Says the Energy Shock Will Be Short-Lived. CEOs Paint a Scarier Picture.

headline: Venezuela oil reserves both entice and repel energy giants
summary:
President Donald Trump's administration is pushing to open Venezuela's untapped natural resources to US investment
but the oil-rich Latin American country's outdated infrastructure is
making energy giants think twice about going all-in. Under pressure from Washington, Venezuela's interim president Delcy Rodriguez pushed through a major reform of the country's hydrocarbon laws in January, opening up the sector to private and foreign investment.

content.title: Venezuela oil reserves both entice and repel energy giants
content.summary:
President Donald Trump's administration is pushing to open Venezuela's untapped natural resources to US investment
but the oil-rich Latin American country's outdated infrastructure is
making energy giants think twice about going all-in. Under pressure from Washington, Venezuela's interim president Delcy Rodriguez pushed through a major reform of the country's hydrocarbon laws in January, opening up the sector to private and foreign investment.

thumbnail.caption: Venezuela has the largest known oil reserves in the world, but years of underinvestment, lack of maintenance and US sanctions have hammered oil production (RONALDO SCHEMIDT)

headline: Energy Transition Update - Malaysia Accelerates Solar Growth with LSS5 Program Expansion
summary: Malaysia's commitment to the energy transition is further underscored by the recent developments under its Large Scale Solar 5 (LSS5) program, which is part of the nation's long-term strategy to achieve 70 percent renewable energy installed capacity and carbon neutrality by 2050. Recently, Founder Group Limited announced an additional contract of approximately US$8.6 million for a 9.5 MW solar facility under this program. The LSS5 initiative highlights Malaysia's efforts to expand its solar...

headline: Oil Prices Jump As Iran Levies Strait Of Hormuz Toll; S&P 500 Falls As LNG Stocks Rise
summary: U.S. crude oil prices jumped overnight as Iran took steps to formalize a toll on Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic, adding to market nerves about potential U.S. military escalation. With President Donald Trump's five-day ceasefire now in its fourth day, the S&P 500 slid, while Chevron and some other oil and gas stocks posted modest gains.

content.title: Oil Prices Jump As Iran Levies Strait Of Hormuz Toll; S&P 500 Falls As LNG Stocks Rise
content.summary: U.S. crude oil prices jumped overnight as Iran took steps to formalize a toll on Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic, adding to market nerves about potential U.S. military escalation. With President Donald Trump's five-day ceasefire now in its fourth day, the S&P 500 slid, while Chevron and some other oil and gas stocks posted modest gains.

headline: CERAWeek 2026: Key takeaways for energy investors

headline: Producers Are Looking Far Afield for Oil. It’s ‘An Old-Fashioned Land Grab.’
summary: Oil and gas companies have been ramping up their search for new opportunities outside the U.S. for quite a while, and the war in Iran will likely accelerate their hunt in far-off regions.  It’s “an old-fashioned land grab,” Bob Fryklund, S&P Global’s chief upstream energy strategist, said of the industry’s rush to acquire new acreage.  “Each company has a different strategy,” he noted while speaking this week at CERAWeek, an annual energy conference in Houston.

content.title: Producers Are Looking Far Afield for Oil. It’s ‘An Old-Fashioned Land Grab.’
content.summary: Oil and gas companies have been ramping up their search for new opportunities outside the U.S. for quite a while, and the war in Iran will likely accelerate their hunt in far-off regions.  It’s “an old-fashioned land grab,” Bob Fryklund, S&P Global’s chief upstream energy strategist, said of the industry’s rush to acquire new acreage.  “Each company has a different strategy,” he noted while speaking this week at CERAWeek, an annual energy conference in Houston.

headline: Hollub’s Tenure as Occidental CEO May Be Ending. It Hasn’t Been Good for Investors.
